title: BLD-401: Improve hlx tickets create ergonomics
description: Walkthrough of changes to fix --repos resolution, add --description-file, and add update-description subcommand

steps.title: New resolveAllRepos() batch function
steps.description: Added resolveAllRepos() that resolves multiple repo names/keys/IDs in a single listRepos() API call. Uses the same matching logic as resolveRepo (exact ID > exact name > partial name). Collects all unresolved entries and throws a single Error with all unknowns listed, letting the caller format the final user-facing message.

steps.title: Description flag handling: mutual exclusivity and file-path detection
steps.description: Replaced requireFlag('--description') with dual-flag logic. getFlag reads both --description and --description-file. Mutual exclusivity is enforced: both present -> error, neither present -> error. When --description-file is given, readFileSync loads the file. When --description is given, accessSync+statSync check if the value is a readable file path — if so, exits with a clear error directing the user to --description-file.

steps.title: Repo resolution via resolveAllRepos
steps.description: Replaced the raw repositoryIds pass-through with resolveAllRepos(config, repoEntries). On failure, prints the error message (listing unknown repos and available repos) plus a reference to 'hlx inspect repos'. This provides client-side validation before any ticket is created.

steps.title: New update-description subcommand handler
steps.description: New file implementing cmdTicketsUpdateDescription. Accepts --file <path> or --text <string> (mutually exclusive). Reads file content via readFileSync when --file is used. Calls hxFetch with PATCH method and basePath '/api' to update the ticket description. Follows the established one-file-per-subcommand pattern.

steps.title: Registered update-description in switch block
steps.description: Added 'update-description' case following the extractTicketRef + resolveTicket + handler pattern used by get, rerun, continue, etc. Also updated ticketsUsage() at line 15 to include the new subcommand and --description-file in the create usage, plus documentation that --repos accepts names/keys/IDs.
